commit.message:
[services] Optimize EventDispatcherAzure with Queue client caching (#984)

* Optimize EventDispatcherAzure with client caching, singleton

Refactored EventDispatcherAzure to cache QueueClient instances using a ConcurrentDictionary, reducing redundant client creation. Changed DI registration to singleton scope for EventDispatcherAzure, ensuring efficient resource usage and improved performance.

* Refactor QueueClient caching for thread safety

Replaced direct QueueClient caching with Lazy<Task<QueueClient>> in a ConcurrentDictionary to ensure thread-safe, one-time initialization per queue. This prevents race conditions and redundant queue creation when accessed concurrently, improving reliability and efficiency in EventDispatcherAzure.

* Refactor QueueClient caching with IQueueClientCache

Extract QueueClient caching logic from EventDispatcherAzure into a new IQueueClientCache interface and QueueClientCache implementation. Update dependency injection to register IQueueClientCache as a singleton and inject it into EventDispatcherAzure, which now requires it in the constructor. Change IEventDispatcher lifetime to transient. Update tests to use a mocked IQueueClientCache. This improves testability, separation of concerns, and ensures efficient, thread-safe QueueClient reuse.

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle faulted queue client tasks in cache

Wrap queue client retrieval in a try-catch block to remove faulted tasks from the cache, allowing retries on subsequent calls instead of reusing failed tasks. Re-throw the exception after cleanup.

* Improve error handling for Azure queue creation failures

Wrap EnsureExistsAsync in a try-catch block to log detailed errors when queue creation or retrieval fails, including queue name and event type. This enhances diagnostics and reliability when dispatching events.

---------

commit.message:
Re-enable symbol stripping for Apple platforms (#124266)

## Description

Re-enables symbol stripping for Apple platforms by removing the
`StripSymbols=false` workarounds from `ILCompiler_publish.csproj`,
`crossgen2_publish.csproj`, `ilasm.csproj`, and
`XUnitLogChecker.csproj`. Apple's pre-built Swift runtime static
libraries contain DWARF debug info with `-gmodules` references to `.pcm`
module cache files that only existed on Apple's build machines, causing
`dsymutil` to output `error:` messages even though it exits with code 0.

To handle this, `IgnoreStandardErrorWarningFormat="true"` is added to
the dsymutil `Exec` tasks in `Microsoft.NETCore.Native.targets`,
`Microsoft.NET.CrossGen.targets`, and `native-library.targets`,
preventing MSBuild from incorrectly counting these output lines as build
errors while still catching real failures via non-zero exit codes.

Additionally, Apple platform detection in `eng/toolAot.targets` is fixed
by using `PortableOS` instead of `_IsApplePlatform` (which is
unavailable at that evaluation point) and broadening the condition to
`UseNativeAotForComponents` to cover components like XUnitLogChecker
that set `PublishAot` directly.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123687

---------

Co-authored-by: Michal Strehovský <MichalStrehovsky@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Increase default epochs to 2000 and add pixel-stable early stopping

Training could stall on corridor-shaped data: once most points were
correctly classified, sigmoid saturation made gradients near-zero,
and 500 epochs wasn't enough for the boundary to finish rotating.

Changes:
- model.py: default epochs 500 -> 2000, add patience=200 kwarg that
  stops training when the quantized pixel bytes are unchanged for 200
  consecutive epochs (keyword-only to preserve positional compat)
- cli.py: match new default epochs
- docs/app.js, docs/index.html: mirror both changes in the browser demo
- tests: add test_early_stopping, update test_training_history for new API

[crossgen2] Add --strip-il-bodies option for composite R2R (#125647)

## Description

Add a `--strip-il-bodies` crossgen2 option that replaces IL method
bodies of successfully R2R-compiled methods with minimal stubs (`ret`)
in composite output assemblies. This reduces assembly size for scenarios
where IL interpreter fallback is not needed, such as full R2R on
iOS/MacCatalyst.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/124860

commit.message:
example: set device cookie httpOnly (#868)

Fix for
[https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5](https://github.com/zitadel/oidc/security/code-scanning/5)

To fix the problem in general, any cookie that stores authentication or
authorization-related data and is not intended to be accessed by
client-side JavaScript should be created with `HttpOnly: true`. In Go,
this is done by setting the `HttpOnly` field on the `http.Cookie`
struct.

In this specific file, the cookie is created in `loginHandler` at lines
164–169. We should update the `http.Cookie` literal to include
`HttpOnly: true`. This leaves the rest of the behavior unchanged: same
name, value, path, and use of `securecookie` for encoding. No other code
changes are required, and no new imports are needed, because `HttpOnly`
is already part of the `net/http` standard library’s `Cookie` struct.

Concretely:
- In `example/server/exampleop/device.go`, locate the cookie creation in
`loginHandler`.
- Modify the struct literal for `cookie` to add `HttpOnly: true`.
- Keep `Name`, `Value`, and `Path` as they are.

This single change will address all three CodeQL alert variants, since
they all refer to the same cookie.


_Suggested fixes powered by Copilot Autofix. Review carefully before
merging._

Fix ReboxLocal missing from bytecode merge/adjust and eval for-loop local visibility

Three locations scanned eval/module bytecode to skip operands but did not
account for ReboxLocal (opcode 109, 1-byte operand):

1. merge_eval_chunk in vm.rs — bytecode copier that adjusts constant
   indices and jump targets when merging eval code into the host chunk.
2. Non-configurable property check scanner in vm.rs — pre-scan of eval
   bytecode to detect illegal redefinitions.
3. adjust_merged_bytecode in opcode.rs — module bytecode IP/constant
   offset adjuster.

Missing ReboxLocal caused its 1-byte operand to be interpreted as the
next opcode, shifting all subsequent bytes and corrupting the entire
code stream. This manifested as panics (index out of bounds, unhandled
opcode 109) when eval or module code contained for-let loops.

Also extended top_level_locals_at_ip snapshots to cover all scope depths
at function_depth==0 (not just scope_depth==0), and added snapshot
recording at for-loop init entry/exit. This allows eval inside a
top-level for(let ...) body to see the loop variable.

Fixes: tagged-template/cache-eval-inner-function (cluster 15)
Fixes: module-code/namespace/define-own-property (clusters 10, 18)

owner)

project A
9. 🟢 [on PR] Notice that "Select source project…" dropdown lists all
your projects
10. Select project A
11. Click "copy" and then confirm with "PROCEED"
12. 🟢 Notice a success notification "permissions were copied
successfully"

from point (4)

Fix mobile startup and player layout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix eval lex-env-heritage for block-scoped locals at top level

Direct eval inside a block at the top level now correctly sees
block-scoped let/const variables. The TDZ fix (force_local_let)
stored these as stack locals + BoxLocal cells, but the eval handler
only walked CallFrame locals and missed top-level block-scoped ones.

Added top_level_locals_at_ip snapshots to Chunk: the compiler records
the active local names (with const tracking) at block entry/exit at
scope_depth 0. The eval handler finds the latest snapshot before the
current IP and injects those locals into the eval VM by reading from
top_level_cells or the stack.

Fixes: language/eval-code/direct/lex-env-heritage.js
